doc_title: Exploring Equilibrium Strategies in Network Games with Generative AI

items.evidence_span: [7] utilizes intelligent agent-based modeling and simulation (ABMS) to depict player behavior.
[8] has examined the efficacy of a discriminative AI-based approach.
[9] proposes a mathematical mixed model that reflects players' benefits and objectives.
[2] designs a variablesum two-player sequential Stackelberg game.
specifically, FBM can be used in the multi-agent continuous control scenario [11].
Unlike the Nash equilibrium, the proximal equilibrium captures the sequential nature of GAN, where the generator acts first, followed by the discriminator [4].
when the channel allocation problem for ultradense cloud D2D communication networks with asymmetric interference is modeled as a combinational optimization problem [5],
TBM can be integrated into reinforcement learning [6], offering decision-making support.
This framework offers emulation, evaluation, and optimization services for physical entities (e.g., players of one game).
For instance, [3] proposed a framework leveraging LLMs to create agents capable of reasoning and decision-makings, providing final response to the input requirement.
Employing a generative diffusion model-based reinforcement learning approach, their optimization objective is to maximize the sum rate of all channels.
For instance, [6] leverages LLMs to streamline software development processes, automating tasks such as testing, debugging, and deployment.
To address such a concern, [13] utilizes GAN to design games between the attacker and defender in training and deployment phases.
